Union Government Constitutes Search-cum-Selection Committee for National Sports Board Under NSG Act, 2025
articles.full_content:
Cabinet Secretary Dr. T.V. Somanathan to Head 5-Member National Sports Board Search-cum-Selection Committee
The Union Government today constituted the Search-cum Selection Committee for National Sports Board under National Sports Board (Search-cum Selection Committee) Rules, 2026 under the National Sports Governance Act, 2025.
The Search-cum Selection Committee will be headed by Dr. T.V. Somanathan, Cabinet Secretary as Chairperson and will consist of Shri Hari Ranjan Rao, Secretary (Sports), Lt General (Retd) Harpal Singh (Indian Olympic Association), Sports Administrator, and two National Sports Awardees namely Shri Gagan Narang and Ms. N. Kunjurani Devi.
The aforementioned committee is tasked to recommend a panel of names for the positions of Chairperson and Two Members of the Board, from among persons of ability, integrity and standing, having knowledge or practical experience in the field of public administration, sports governance, sports law and other related fields.
The constitution of Search-cum-Selection Committee by the Ministry will set in motion the process of selection of Chairperson and Members of the National Sports Board, which will act as the central authority responsible for granting board recognition to National Sports Bodies and ensuring compliance with governance, financial and ethical standards.

Union Minister for MSME Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi and Bihar Chief Minister Shri Samrat Choudhary Lay Foundation Stone for Technology Centre, Gaya Ji
articles.full_content:
Ministry of MSME is creating significant employment opportunities and strengthening the foundation of self-reliance: Bihar CM Technology Centre to support General Engineering, Heavy Engineering and Textile Testing Aims to train 7,000 trainees annually through short-term and long-term industry-oriented programmes
Union Minister for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi and the Chief Minister of Bihar, Shri Samrat Choudhary, laid the Foundation Stone for the upcoming Technology Centre at Khizarsarai, Gaya Ji, Bihar.
The ceremony commenced with a traditional Bhoomi Pujan (ground-breaking ceremony), marking the beginning of a landmark project aimed at strengthening the MSME ecosystem and industrial infrastructure in the region. The event was attended by senior officials from the Ministry of MSME and the Ministers from Government of Bihar, along with representatives from industry and local stakeholders.
Union Minister for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi highlighted the importance of TC Gayaji in providing technology support to MSMEs of not only Bihar but also neighbouring States. He said that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is working towards establishing technology-driven centres across the country.
He also stated that what once seemed impossible has been made possible with the establishment of the Technology Centre in Gaya Ji. The centre is expected to train around 7,000 trainees and will play a crucial role in skill development and employment generation.
Chief Minister of Bihar, Shri Samrat Choudhary thanked the public and for guests present on the auspicious occasion of the foundation stone laying ceremony. He expressed his gratitude to the Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi for providing the opportunity to establish such Technology Centres across the country. He said that it was a matter of pride to inaugurate a centre that would play a transformative role in skill development and employment generation.
Highlighting the contribution of the MSME sector, he said that the Ministry of MSME is creating significant employment opportunities and strengthening the foundation of self-reliance. He emphasized that this is the time for Bihar to transform and move forward with determination and hard work. He remarked that the vision of a Viksit Bharat can be achieved only when people gain employment and enhance their skills through technology and innovation.
Shri Bharat Khera, Secretary, Ministry of MSME stated that the Ministry of MSME is establishing Technology Centres and Extension Centres across various States/UTs of the country. Recently, TC at Bihar, Patna was inaugurated and now TC at Gaya ji will be established.
Dr. Rajneesh, Additional Secretary and Development Commissioner, Ministry of MSME, outlined the progress made in MSME sector in India and provided an overview of the MSME sector in Bihar and the Technology Centre (TC), Gaya Ji.
The Technology Centre, Gaya Ji, will be established at Khizarsarai, Gaya J

PERIODIC LABOUR FORCE SURVEY (PLFS) MONTHLY BULLETIN - May, 2026
articles.full_content:
LFPR for urban female maintains stability Urban WPR broadly stable Urban UR falls to one-year low
• Overall LFPR (15+ years) recorded as 54.4% in May, 2026, compared to 55.0% in April, 2026 and 54.8% in May, 2025.
• Female LFPR in urban areas largely stable compared to the previous month.
• Overall WPR (15+ years) stood at 51.4% May, 2026, compared to 52.2% in April, 2026 and 51.7% in May, 2025.
• Urban WPR (15+ years) broadly stable at 46.6% compared to the previous month.
• Overall UR (15+ years) in urban areas declined to 6.4% in May, 2026 while Female UR (15+ years) in urban areas declined to 8.2% in May, 2026.
• UR for urban male (15+ years) stood at 5.9%, maintaining the same level of April, 2026.
The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) conducted by NSO, MoSPI, is the primary source of data on activity participation and employment and unemployment conditions of the population. The PLFS survey methodology has been modified from January, 2025 to provide monthly and quarterly estimates of labour force indicators for the country.
Monthly results of PLFS are released in the form of Monthly Bulletins. It presents estimates of key labour market indicators viz. Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR), Worker Population Ratio (WPR) and Unemployment Rate (UR) at the all-India level following the Current Weekly Status(CWS) approach.
The Monthly Bulletins for April, 2025 to April, 2026 have already been released. The present Monthly Bulletin for May, 2026 is the fourteenth in the series.
______________________
1. The activity status of a person is determined on the basis of the activities pursued by the person during the specified reference period. The activity status determined on the basis of a reference period of last 7 days preceding the date of survey is known as the Current Weekly Status (CWS) of the person.
Key findings for persons of age 15 years and above following CWS:
• Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) moderates marginally:
The Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) for persons aged 15 years and above was reported as 54.4% in May, 2026, compared to 55.0% in April, 2026. In rural and urban areas, LFPR was recorded at 56.6% and 49.8%, respectively.
The year-on-year comparison shows that the overall LFPR in May, 2026 declined by 0.4 percentage points. Rural and urban LFPR also registered a decline of 0.3 and 0.6 percentage points, respectively, over the same period.
In May, 2026, LFPR stood at 54.4% against 54.8% in May, 2025
• Female labour force participation broadly stable in urban areas:
Female Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) for age 15 years and above stood at 32.8% in May, 2026. In rural areas, female LFPR was recorded at 36.7%, whereas it remained almost at the same level at 24.8% in urban areas compared to the previous month.

First Phase of Census 2027 Successfully Completed in Maharashtra
articles.full_content:
Mumbai : 15 June 2026
The first phase of Census 2027 — House Listing and Housing Census — has been successfully completed in Maharashtra. The exercise was conducted across the State from 16 May 2026 to 14 June 2026.
During this phase, Enumerators visited households in the House Listing Blocks assigned to them and collected responses through a mobile application on 33 questions notified by the Government of India.
The questions pertained to the condition of houses, amenities available to households, and household assets. All personal information provided by households in connection with the Census is legally protected and will remain strictly confidential.
Apart from generating information on housing conditions, household amenities, and availability of assets, the House Listing exercise undertaken during this phase will also provide the framework for the Population Enumeration scheduled in February 2027, thereby ensuring complete coverage.
Phase-I of Census 2027 in Maharashtra was conducted across all 36 districts and 29 Municipal Corporations, for which Principal Census Officers were appointed. A total of 2,20,514 House Listing Blocks were created and assigned to individual Enumerators for smooth and systematic enumeration. Each group of six Enumerators was supervised by one Supervisor.
To ensure timely completion of the exercise, 63 Principal Census Officers comprising 34 District Collectors and 29 Municipal Commissioners, 947 Charge Officers, 141 Master Trainers, 3,850 Field Trainers, and around 2.45 lakh Enumerators and Supervisors were deployed across the State.
The last Census of India was conducted in 2011. Census 2027 will be the 16th Census in the country and the 8th since Independence.
The Directorate of Census Operations, Maharashtra expressed sincere gratitude to all Enumerators, Supervisors, and other Census functionaries who worked tirelessly for the successful completion of the exercise within the stipulated timeline. The Directorate also thanked the residents of the State, the Government of Maharashtra, District Administrations, Municipal Corporations, and media organisations for their valuable cooperation and support during the House Listing and Housing Census phase of Census 2027.
PIB Mumbai | Saurabh Khekde/ Priti Malandkar

The exercise was conducted across the State from 16 May 2026 to 14 June 2026. During this phase, Enumerators visited households in the House Listing Blocks assigned to them and collected responses through a mobile application on 33 questions notified by the Government of India.

Union Minister of State Shri V. Somanna Highlights 12 Years of Transformative Governance and Unprecedented Achievements under PM Shri Narendra Modi
articles.full_content:
"The Middle Class in India is no longer just aspirational, it is on the rise, driving the vision of Viksit Bharat @ 2047," says Shri V. Somanna
Shri V. Somanna, Hon'ble Minister of State for Railways and Jal Shakti, Government of India, and Member of Parliament from Tumakuru, addressed the media today, detailing the monumental achievements of the Government of India over the past 12 years (2014–2026) under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi.
Summarizing the 12-year journey, MoS Shri V. Somanna stated, "The Middle Class in India is no longer just aspirational; it is on the rise. Flagship schemes like PM Awas, Ujjwala, Ayushman Bharat, and Jan Dhan have provided our citizens with dignity, security, and savings. Today, MSMEs and Startups are the new economic engines for our districts. The vision of 'Viksit Bharat @ 2047' is dedicated to fulfilling the dreams of those aspiring for their first car, their first flat, and higher education for their children."
Highlighting a decade-plus of inclusive growth, infrastructure development, and cultural renaissance, Shri Somanna emphasized how these transformative policies have elevated the lives of every citizen, particularly farmers, women, youth, and the marginalized.
World's Largest Social Transformation and Poverty Eradication
The Minister noted that over 25 crore people have been lifted above the poverty line, forming India's new rising Middle Class. The government has ensured food security through the PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana, providing free food grains to over 81 crore Indians. Housing and financial inclusion have seen massive strides, with 4+ crore pucca houses constructed under PM Awas Yojana and a banking revolution sparked by the opening of over 58 crore Jan Dhan bank accounts.
Sanitation, Water, and Basic Infrastructure
Detailing the focus on dignity and health, Shri Somanna highlighted the construction of over 12 crore toilets, leading to 5 lakh villages being declared Open Defecation Free (ODF). The Jal Jeevan Mission has successfully delivered tap water connections to more than 16 crore households. Furthermore, the PM Ujjwala Yojana has created "smoke-free kitchens" by providing 11 crore LPG connections to women.
Empowering Entrepreneurs & The Rising Middle Class
To boost self-employment, the MUDRA Yojana has sanctioned loans to 57 crore beneficiaries. Special initiatives like PM SVANidhi (loans to 74 lakh street vendors), Stand-Up India (₹15,000 crore loans for SC/ST entrepreneurs), and PM Vishwakarma (supporting 30 lakh traditional artisans) have empowered millions at the grassroots. Additionally, the ambitious Rooftop Solar initiative targets 1+ crore installations to provide free electricity to households.
Farmers’ Welfare and Agricultural Boom

Current Place of Residence Must Be Reported During House Listing, Says Census Nodal Officer
The Self-Enumeration Portal for Phase I of Census 2027, India's first digital census, will be open in Kerala from 6:00 a.m. on June 16, 2026, said Shri K. Biju, Secretary of the General Administration Department, Government of Kerala, and State Nodal Officer (Census).
Speaking at a press conference in Thiruvananthapuram regarding Phase I of the Census in Kerala, Shri Biju clarified that respondents must provide information based on their current place of residence during the first phase, which includes house listing and housing census operations. People can directly submit their household information through the self-enumeration portal at https://se.census.gov.in/ . Students and employees residing away from their permanent homes for education or work should report the location where they are currently staying. Likewise, tenants should provide details of the rented house in which they presently reside.
To complete self-enumeration, individuals must log in to the portal using their mobile number and other required details. After all questions are completed, a Self-Enumeration ID (SEID) will be generated and sent to the registered mobile number. This SEID must be shared with the enumerator during the field visit. After verifying the self-reported information, the enumerator will upload the data to the Census database.
Shri Biju noted that even if residents are unable to complete self-enumeration, enumerators will collect the required information through field visits between July 1 and July 30. He added that self-enumeration is expected to make the census process more efficient. Emphasizing the importance of the exercise, he said that the Census plays a crucial role in national development and resource allocation.
Speaking on the occasion, Dr. T. Mithra, Director of Census Operations and Chief Principal Census Officer, said that individual information collected during the Census would not be made public. Only aggregated data would be released, she said, assuring citizens that the information provided would remain secure and that privacy would be protected. Dr. Mithra also stated that the training of enumerators would be completed by June 23. Each enumerator has been assigned approximately 150 to 200 households. Data collection will be carried out through a mobile application available in 16 languages.
Dr. Mithra further informed that a web-based portal named the Census Management and Monitoring System (CMMS) will be used to manage and monitor various activities related to Census 2027. For queries related to Census information, a toll-free helpline number, 1855, has been established.
Also present at the press conference were Thiruvananthapuram District Collector Anu Kumari, IAS, and Dr. Athira Thampi, Deputy Director of the Press Information Bureau, Thiruvananthapuram.
articles.summary: Current Place of Residence Must Be Reported During House Listing, Says Census Nodal Officer The Self-Enumeration Portal for Phase I of Census 2027, India's first digital census, will be open in Kerala from 6:00 a.m. Biju, Secretary of the General Administration Department, Government of Kerala, and State Nodal Officer (Census). Speaking at a press conference in Thiruvananthapuram regarding Phase I of the Census in Kerala, Shri Biju clarified that respondents must provide information based on their current place of residence during the first phase, which includes house listing and housing census operations.

PRESS RELEASE ON NEW SERIES OF WHOLESALE PRICE INDEX, AND PRODUCER PRICE INDICES WITH BASE YEAR 2022-23
articles.full_content:
The Office of Economic Adviser, DPIIT is releasing the revised series of WPI with base year 2022-23. The new series will replace the existing series of WPI with base year 2011-12. In addition, the Office is also releasing new series of Output Producer Price Index (OPPI), Trial Input Producer Price Index (IPPI), and Service Producer Price Index (Service PPI) of seven services, viz., Banking, Securities Transaction, Insurance, Management of Pension Funds, Railways, Air (Passenger), and Telecom with the same base year.
Considering the wide usage of WPI in price escalation clauses, this index will be released for five years from the date of its release, along with PPI, and will be discontinued thereafter. This would give sufficient time to users to switch from WPI to PPI. The transition from WPI to PPI is in alignment with the global best practices adopted by advanced economies and the recommendations of the International Monetary Fund (IMF). Availability of both the Output PPI and Input PPI gives a better understanding of the price movements of output produced vis-a-vis inputs being used in an industry. It also explains how inflation experienced by producers on inputs are passed on to the output being produced.
Service PPIs for seven services have been compiled in the first phase based on the availability of data from administrative sources/agencies.
WPI, and Output PPI, being compiled on a monthly basis, are being released for the month of May 2026 (Provisional Estimates). The back series of these indices are placed on the web portal of the Office of the Economic Adviser at weblink ( https://eaindustry.nic.in/download_data_2223.asp ). The monthly Trial Input PPI (only for Manufacturing Sector) is being published on an experimental basis March 2026 onwards. The publication of Trial Input PPI would enable the Department to examine the data quality and also receive feedback of stakeholders and users.
Service PPIs, compiled on a quarterly basis, are being released for Q4 of 2025-26 (Provisional). The back series of Service PPIs is placed on the web portal of the Office of the Economic Adviser at weblink (https://eaindustry.nic.in/download_data_2223.asp ).
Key features of the new WPI series (Base Year 2022-23):
• Increased number of Items : The total number of items have increased from 697 to 957.
• Inclusion of New and Renewable Energy under Electricity: New sources of energy, such as Solar and Wind, have been added under ‘Electricity’ Group. In addition, Nuclear Electricity has been included in the basket.
• Coherent and Integrated Structure for Tracking Energy Prices: Crude Petroleum and Natural Gas has been shifted from ‘Primary Articles’ to ‘Fuel and Power’ major group. This reorganization has been done to ensure better alignment, as this group already houses other major fuels such as coal, electricity, and petroleum products.
• Improved Methodology for Derivation of Weights: Gross Value of Output (GVO) has been used for preparing weights for WPI 2
articles.summary: The Office of Economic Adviser, DPIIT is releasing the revised series of WPI with base year 2022-23. The new series will replace the existing series of WPI with base year 2011-12. In addition, the Office is also releasing new series of Output Producer Price Index (OPPI), Trial Input Producer Price Index (IPPI), and Service Producer Price Index (Service PPI) of seven services, viz., Banking, Securities Transaction, Insurance, Management of Pension Funds, Railways, Air (Passenger), and Telecom with the same base year.

Common Recruitment Exam to fill AIIMS posts
articles.full_content:
All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi has issued a Common Recruitment Examination (CRE-5) notification to fill various vacancies. The detailed recruitment advertisement is meant for recruitment in 32 different participating AIIMS and Central Government Institutes/bodies, including AIIMS Mangalarigi.
The online registration process is active for Common Recruitment Examination, and the last date for submitting online applications is 3rd July 2026, up to 5:00 PM. Eligible Indian nationals must apply strictly through the Online Registration (OTR) system on the official website www.aiimsexams.ac.in, only. The site also provides further details for the candidates. AIIMS New Delhi is conducting the examination without prior scrutiny of eligibility. Candidature remains purely provisional, if an applicant is found ineligible at any stage, their candidature will be structurally disqualified or cancelled. Candidates must exercise extreme care while filing the online application.
The selection process will rely on a 90 minutes Computer Based Test (CBT) consist of 100 multiple-choice questions, tentatively scheduled to take place between 25th July and 27th July 2026. AIIMS Mangalagiri located in Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh, is officially listed as a participating institution for CRE-5. The specific non-faculty posts advertised for AIIMS Mangalagiri.

ASI and National Museum of Denmark Signed MoU for Underwater Archaeological Investigation of Historic Danish Ship Oresund
articles.full_content:
The Archaeological Survey of India, Underwater Archaeology Wing and the National Museum of Denmark: Njord - Center for Maritime and Underwater Cultural Heritage, Copenhagen, have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to undertake a collaborative underwater archaeological project aimed at locating and documenting the remains of the historic Danish ship Oresund , which was wrecked off the coast near Karaikal, Puducherry, in 1619 CE.
The Oresund occupies a significant place in maritime history as the first Danish ship known to have reached India. Shortly after its arrival in Indian waters, the vessel was wrecked near Karaikal, making it an important archaeological resource for understanding the early maritime interactions between Denmark and India, as well as the broader history of seafaring and trade in the Indian Ocean during the early seventeenth century.
Under the terms of the MoU, the project will focus on conducting a non-invasive archaeological survey using advanced technologies and scientific methods to identify potential remains of the shipwreck. The investigations will be carried out by the Underwater Archaeology Wing of the Archaeological Survey of India in collaboration with the National Museum of Denmark.
This collaboration marks a significant milestone for the Underwater Archaeology Wing, as it represents its first collaborative archaeological project with an international organisation. The partnership is expected to strengthen academic and scientific cooperation between India and Denmark in the field of underwater cultural heritage research. This initiative also reflects the shared commitment of both institutions towards the documentation, protection, and study of underwater cultural heritage through international cooperation and the application of non-invasive scientific methodologies.

The Oresund occupies a significant place in maritime history as the first Danish ship known to have reached India. Bihar delegation explores Punjab’s rich heritage, governance and urban planning excellence under Yuva Sangam Phase VI
articles.full_content:
A student delegation from Bihar visited Punjab as part of the Yuva Sangam Phase VI programme. The visit concluded last Saturday (June 13, 2026). Led by the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Patna, the participants had embarked on an educational and cultural immersion visit to Punjab, hosted by the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Ropar.
The delegation also called on the Governor of Punjab, Shri Gulab Chand Kataria, at Lok Bhavan, Chandigarh, last Wednesday (June 10, 2026). During the interaction, the Governor encouraged the participants to embrace India’s cultural diversity, contribute actively towards nation-building and strengthen the spirit of national unity. He emphasized the importance of youth participation in shaping the future of the country and fostering harmony across regions. The session offered valuable insights into leadership, public service and responsible citizenship.
During their stay, the delegates visited several iconic landmarks of Punjab and Chandigarh, gaining first-hand exposure to the region’s cultural heritage, urban planning achievements and historical significance. The participants visited the renowned Sukhna Lake, one of Chandigarh’s most prominent landmarks and an integral part of the city’s planned development. The visit enabled delegates to appreciate the harmonious integration of natural landscapes with urban design and public spaces.
The delegation also visited the historic Harappan archaeological site at Ropar (Rupnagar), situated along the banks of the Sutlej River. The site provided participants with valuable insights into the country’s ancient civilisation and archaeological heritage. The delegation also visited the ancestral house of Bhagat Singh.
As part of the experiential learning component of Yuva Sangam, the delegates explored Chandigarh’s iconic Sector 17 Plaza and other urban landmarks, gaining insights into the vision and planning principles that shaped India’s first planned city. The participants observed how thoughtful urban design, public infrastructure and civic spaces contribute to sustainable and inclusive development.
The Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education launched Yuva Sangam Phase VI initiative under the Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat programme with the objective of fostering national integration and strengthening people-to-people connections among the youth of the country. The programme provides participants from 22 paired States and Union Territories with opportunities to engage with India’s diverse cultural traditions, educational institutions, innovation ecosystems and developmental initiatives, thereby deepening appreciation for the nation’s unity amidst diversity.

Led by the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Patna, the participants had embarked on an educational and cultural immersion visit to Punjab, hosted by the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Ropar. The delegation also called on the Governor of Punjab, Shri Gulab Chand Kataria, at Lok Bhavan, Chandigarh, last Wednesday (June 10, 2026).

Wind Power to Drive India’s Clean Energy Future and Viksit Bharat Vision: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi
articles.full_content:
India records highest-ever annual wind capacity addition of over 6 GW in 2025-26; targets 100 GW wind capacity by 2030: Joshi
Panaji-Goa : 15 June 2026
Union Minister of New and Renewable Energy, Shri Pralhad Joshi, today said that India’s clean energy transition is driving the nation’s journey towards Viksit Bharat and that the country’s wind energy sector is entering a new era of global leadership powered by innovation, exports and energy security.
Addressing the media during the Global Wind Day Conference 2026 held in Goa, Shri Joshi highlighted the remarkable progress achieved by India’s renewable energy sector under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi over the past twelve years.
The Union Minister stated that Goa hosting the national Global Wind Day celebrations reflects the State’s growing role in India’s renewable energy transition. He appreciated the active participation of the Government of Goa in advancing the country’s green growth agenda.
Shri Joshi said that India’s wind sector has witnessed unprecedented growth, with annual wind turbine manufacturing capacity increasing from 10 GW in 2014 to 24 GW today, reflecting the success of the Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative. He informed that India recorded its highest-ever annual wind capacity addition in 2025-26, crossing 6 GW in a single year, representing nearly a 46 per cent increase over the previous year and reaffirming India’s position among the world’s fastest-growing wind energy markets.
Emphasizing the importance of wind energy in the evolving energy landscape, the Minister said that wind energy is no longer merely a source of power generation but is increasingly becoming a reliability resource. The future of renewable energy, he noted, lies in integrated systems combining wind, solar energy, energy storage and advanced forecasting technologies to deliver affordable and reliable 24x7 clean power.
Shri Joshi pointed out that a wind-rich power system could potentially save the country nearly ₹2.3 lakh crore, demonstrating the economic benefits of wind energy beyond electricity generation. He further highlighted that India’s wind energy potential remains enormous and largely untapped. At a hub height of 150 metres, the country’s estimated wind energy potential stands at nearly 1,164 GW, making it one of the largest renewable energy opportunities in the world.
The Minister informed that India is targeting 100 GW of installed wind energy capacity by 2030 and 156 GW by 2036, making wind power a critical pillar of the country’s clean energy transition.
Referring to the achievements of the Modi Government, Shri Joshi said that Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi has become India’s longest-serving Prime Minister and one of the longest-serving leaders among major democracies. He noted that the last twelve years have been marked by rapid transformation across sectors.

Ayushman Bharat and Maternal Health Initiatives highlighted at Media Workshop Vartalaap in Kharagpur
articles.full_content:
Nearly 32 lakh Beneficiaries in Paschim Medinipur to get Benefit from Ayushman Bharat Scheme Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan to strengthen Maternal Healthcare Services in Paschim Medinipur
Kharagpur, June 15, 2026
Press Information Bureau (PIB), Kolkata, organised a media workshop Vartalaap at Kharagpur, Paschim Medinipur today, to create awareness about flagship healthcare initiatives of the Union Government and their implementation at the grassroots level.
Addressing the programme, Chief Medical Officer of Health (CMOH), Paschim Medinipur, Dr. Saumya Sankar Sarangi highlighted the benefits of the Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) and the forthcoming implementation of the Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan (PMSMA) in West Bengal.
Dr. Sarangi informed that nearly 32 lakh residents of Paschim Medinipur district are expected to benefit from the Ayushman Bharat scheme, while approximately 6 crore people across West Bengal will be covered under the programme.
The CMOH explained the eligibility criteria and beneficiary categories under the scheme, noting that eligible families will receive cashless healthcare benefits of up to ₹5 lakh per family per year through empanelled hospitals.
The scheme aims to provide financial protection against major health expenditures and ensure access to quality healthcare services for economically vulnerable families.
The CMOH stated that Ayushman Bharat enrollment facilities are being made available through Jan Kalyan Shivirs from June 15 onwards to facilitate registration and awareness among eligible beneficiaries. He added that Accredited Social Health Activists (ASHA workers) and Common Service Centres (CSCs) will play an important role in conducting household outreach and assisting citizens with enrollment and documentation.
To ensure smooth implementation, healthcare workers and local administration have been mobilised to reach beneficiaries at the community level.
Dr. Sarangi informed that the Ayushman Bharat services in the district will initially be operational through two designated model hospitals from July 1, with plans for gradual expansion of service delivery mechanisms.
Beneficiaries will be able to avail themselves of treatment at empanelled healthcare institutions under the scheme without incurring out-of-pocket expenditure, stated CMOH.
Highlighting the importance of maternal healthcare, the CMOH announced that the Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan will also be implemented in the State to strengthen healthcare support for pregnant women.
Under the programme, expectant mothers will receive:
- Free health check-ups
- Diagnostic services
- Essential medicines
- Regular medical monitoring and counselling
ASHA workers will conduct household surveys to identify pregnant women, facilitate registration and guide them throughout the pregnancy period.

House Listing and Housing Census Phase of Census 2027 Successfully Completed in Punjab
articles.full_content:
The House Listing and Housing Census (Phase-I) of the Census of India 2027 has been successfully completed across the State of Punjab during the notified period from 15th May 2026 to 13th June 2026. The Operation was carried out under the guidance of the Chief Secretary, Punjab, and with the support of the Department of Local Government, Punjab.
During this phase, Enumerators visited allotted House Listing Blocks in all villages and urban wards across the State and collected information relating to the building, census house and household through a specially designed mobile application notified by the Office of the Registrar General and Census Commissioner, New Delhi, Government of India, covering important aspects such as housing conditions, availability of household amenities and ownership of assets.
Apart from generating comprehensive data on housing conditions and household amenities, the House Listing Operation provides the essential framework for the subsequent Population Enumeration Phase-II of Census 2027, thereby ensuring complete coverage of all households and persons.
The Phase-1 Operation was conducted throughout Punjab's 23 districts and 14 Municipal Corporations, for which Deputy Commissioners and Corporation Commissioners were appointed as Principal Census Officers. A total of 52,564 House Listing Blocks were created and assigned to Enumerators to facilitate systematic and efficient enumeration. In addition, sufficient numbers of Supervisors were appointed to ensure effective monitoring and quality control.
For the successful execution of this nationwide exercise in Punjab, extensive administrative and field-level arrangements were made.
The Census of India is conducted under the provisions of the Census Act, 1948 and the Census Rules, 1990, as amended from time to time. The previous Census was conducted in 2011. Census 2027 will be the 16th Census in India and the 8th Census since Independence.
On the successful completion of the House Listing and Housing Census phase, Dr. Navjot Khosa, IAS, the Chief Principal Census Officer & Director of Census Operations, Punjab, has expressed sincere gratitude to all residents of the State for their wholehearted cooperation and active participation in this nationally important exercise. Appreciation and congratulations are also extended to all Charge Officers, Trainers, Supervisors, Enumerators and other officers and staff whose dedication and hard work ensured the successful completion of Phase-I of Census 2027 in Punjab.
The Census Organisation looks forward to receiving the same enthusiastic support and cooperation from the public during the forthcoming Population Enumeration Phase of Census 2027.
Click for Hindi version
articles.summary: The House Listing and Housing Census (Phase-I) of the Census of India 2027 has been successfully completed across the State of Punjab during the notified period from 15th May 2026 to 13th June 2026. The Operation was carried out under the guidance of the Chief Secretary, Punjab, and with the support of the Department of Local Government, Punjab. During this phase, Enumerators visited allotted House Listing Blocks in all villages and urban wards across the State and collected information relating to the building, census house and household through a specially designed mobile application notified by the Office of the Registrar General and Census Commissioner, New Delhi, Government of India, covering important aspects such as housing conditions, availability of household amenities and ownership of assets.

Addressing a press conference here, Dr Jitendra Singh said the transformation of India’s space sector reflects a larger change taking place across the country, where science and technology have moved beyond laboratories to become part of the national consciousness. India–France ATL Bridge to Strengthen Bilateral Innovation Cooperation through School Innovation Labs
articles.full_content:
Landmark initiative by Atal Innovation Mission & Dassault Systèmes connects young innovators in India and France through a shared innovation ecosystem LoI signed in Nice, France, on the sidelines of Bharat Innovates 2026, inaugurated by Hon'ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and President Emmanuel Macron
France/Delhi: Guided by the shared resolve of Hon’ble Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President of the French Republic, Emmanuel Macron, to deepen innovation-led cooperation between India and France, Atal Innovation Mission (AIM), NITI Aayog, and La Fondation Dassault Systèmes have established the India–France ATL Bridge to enable collaboration among young innovators in both countries.
The initiative establishes the first School Innovation Lab in France, inspired by India’s Atal Tinkering Lab (ATL) framework, creating a structured platform for young learners to collaborate, strengthen linkages between innovation ecosystems, and deepen bilateral innovation cooperation between the two countries.
The Letter of Intent (LoI) formalizing this collaboration was exchanged between Mission Director, Atal Innovation Mission, NITI Aayog, Deepak Bagla and Managing Director, La Fondation Dassault Systèmes Ms. Marie-Pierre Aulas in France today.
The India–France ATL Bridge will serve as a strategic platform for fostering cross-border innovation programmes, entrepreneurial exchanges, and deeper collaboration between the innovation ecosystems of India and France. Through knowledge exchange, capacity building, and joint innovation activities, the ATL Bridge will enable young innovators to co-create solutions to real-world challenges and strengthen early-stage innovation capabilities. By strengthening connections among young innovators, educators, and stakeholders, the initiative aims to further cultivate a vibrant culture of innovation and drive mutually beneficial partnerships between India and France.
The India–France ATL Bridge initiative is being undertaken during the India–France Year of Innovation 2026, reflecting the shared commitment of both nations to strengthen innovation-led cooperation and nurture future-ready talent. The initiative represents a significant step towards expanding international collaboration in school-level innovation and fostering meaningful exchanges among students, educators, and innovation ecosystems in both countries.
Rooted in India’s civilizational philosophy of Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam-“the world is one family, the initiative reflects the belief that knowledge, innovation, and progress are most meaningful when shared. Through the exchange of the Atal Tinkering Lab (ATL) methodology and innovation ecosystem approach, India is extending not only a proven innovation model but also a spirit of partnership, friendship, and collective growth.

Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 to Kick Off in New Delhi from June 15th to 17th, 2026
articles.full_content:
A Landmark Moment in Youth-Led Nation-Building and Governance
Mumbai, 14 June 2026
The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports is all set to host the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 in New Delhi from June 15 th to 17 th , 2026 through MY Bharat – an autonomous body, under the leadership of Union Minister of Youth Affairs & Sports and Labour & Employment, Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya. Building on the success of previous editions, this flagship initiative reimagines youth engagement with democratic processes, offering young citizens a hands-on understanding of governance, policy deliberation, and parliamentary functioning.
The Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament will be inaugurated by Shri Om Birla, Hon'ble Speaker Lok Sabha at Central Hall of Parliament, New Delhi on 16 th June, 2026. Dr Mansukha Mandaviya, Hon'ble Minister for Youth Affairs and Sports, GoI will grace the occasion.
The programme unfolds across three progressive stages, each designed to deepen youth participation:
District Nodal Rounds:
• Wide-ranging deliberations on 50 years of Emergency : Lessons for Indian Democracy were conducted across 757 Nodal Universities/Institutions, bringing the conversation to every corner of the country.
• Top 5 participants from each district got the chance to participate at State Rounds.
• Conducted in State legislative assemblies and prominent government venues across all the States, these rounds offered youth a meaningful glimpse into regional governance structures on the topic Union Budget 2026: Yuva Shakti Driven Budget.
• Presided over by State Speakers and Governors, the sessions elevated the stature of youth voices in public discourse.
National Round (June 15 th –17 th , 2026):
• Top-performing delegates from across all States and Union Territories converge in New Delhi for the grand finale.
• The National Round features a rich programme of activities:
• Opening Remarks: One representative from each team will address the house on the Nari Shakti Vandan : Catalysing Inclusivity and Viksit Bharat 2047 , speaking for 3 minutes each.
• Question Hour: In a true parliamentary spirit, teams will pose and respond to questions — 3 minutes to question, 3 minutes to answer — building towards a collective resolution.
• Masterclass: An Eminent Personality will conduct a masterclass on oratory and public speaking, sharpening the leadership edge of participating youth.
• PM Sangrahalaya Visit: Delegates will explore India's rich political heritage and leadership legacy through a curated visit to the PM Sangrahalaya.
• Visit to the New Parliament: An inspiring walkthrough of India's new Parliament building, offering participants a direct connection to the seat of the nation's democracy.
• Awards Ceremony: The Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 Awards will be presented on June 17 th , recognising outstanding contributions by young participants.
